title: ornithoglossum undulatum var. zeyheri
intro: Ornithoglossum undulatum var. zeyheri is a plant species within the family Colchicaceae, commonly known as the "adder's tongue" due to its unique, tongue-like leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ornithoglossum undulatum var. zeyheri are thought to include the reduction of fever, relief from respiratory issues, and the promotion of wound healing. In some cultures, the plant is prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal properties, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
